excerpts:
Grants - Tailscale Docs
Grants offer significant advantages over traditional access control methods by unifying network and application permissions in a single system. This consolidated approach reduces complexity and provides a more consistent security model across your entire tailnet.
citations.title: Grants - Tailscale Docs

excerpts:
Peer relay handshake never completes on Apple platforms ...
Peer relay sessions are allocated correctly on all platforms, and Linux clients (tailscaled) complete the UDP relay handshake as expected. However, Apple platform clients (macOS Network Extension, iOS, iPadOS) consistently show on the relay server across all sessions, with zero packets and zero bytes transferred.
citations.title: Peer relay handshake never completes on Apple platforms ...

excerpts:
Tailscale Peer Relays · Tailscale Docs
When you configure a device as a peer relay, other devices in the same tailnet can use it to relay traffic for client-to-client connections when a direct connection isn't possible. Tailscale first attempts to use any available peer relays in the tailnet before falling back to DERP servers.
citations.title: Tailscale Peer Relays · Tailscale Docs

MacOS tailscale client and auth key support - Reddit
I ended up just creating another AppleID and inviting that account to my Tailscale network which works fine. I have two older Macs that I can no longer use with my primary AppleID because I activated enhanced data protection.
citations.title: MacOS tailscale client and auth key support - Reddit

excerpts:
Tailscale Peer Relays: High-throughput relays for secure ...
Tailscale Peer Relays is built right into the Tailscale client, and can be enabled with a simple command, using the tailscale set --relay-server-port flag from the Tailscale CLI.
citations.title: Tailscale Peer Relays: High-throughput relays for secure ...

excerpts:
[Feature] Track for supporting Tailscale Peer Relays
Tailscale Peer Relays provides a customer-deployed and managed traffic relaying mechanism. By advertising itself as a peer relay, a Tailscale node can relay traffic for any peer nodes on the tailnet, even for traffic bound to itself.
citations.title: [Feature] Track for supporting Tailscale Peer Relays

excerpts:
Device posture management - Tailscale Docs
If you have an endpoint device posture solution, you may already know which machines in your tailnet do and do not meet your device posture requirements. One approach to ensuring devices on your network comply with your device posture policy is to use the Authorize device API.
citations.title: Device posture management - Tailscale Docs

excerpts:
Tailscale Pricing 2026: Personal & Starter Plans from $6/mo
Tailscale costs $0–$18/user/mo as of June 5, 2026, with 4 plans. Cheap compared to legacy VPN vendors and much easier to manage. Wins on price vs. Cisco or Fortinet VPN setups. The jump from free to paid is a bit steep for small teams. Is Tailscale Worth the Price?
citations.title: Tailscale Pricing 2026: Personal & Starter Plans from $6/mo
